Well, it might be useful to take one minute and explain how we make money, what we're supposed to do to be successful as a venture firm.
So last year we raised a $300 million fund.

A classic venture model says that for every three companies we invest in, one will lose everything, one will kind of get our money back, and one will get five times our money back.
But the reason venture firms are focused on really big ideas is we need to have some big ideas that really, really work in order to drive those kinds of returns.

And the reason we're trying to drive those returns is if it takes us 15 years to return three times our money, if you do the math backwards and you look at things like internal rate of return, that'll generate a rate of return for our investors, the LPs that invest in our funds, of about 20% to 22%.
